title: Delivery Program — Robust Tested Deployment (Phases 0–3)
what_it_is: The full 4-phase Orvex Studio delivery program: Phase 0 Stabilize the ground (fake-done kill switch, gate closure, acceptance re-baseline, open-ticket hygiene, git/worktree cleanup), Phase 1 Definition Factory (per-service PRD delta + frozen tagged contract + test plan + Service Done Definition + agent build prompt, authored fully-autonomously with adversarial self-certification), Phase 2 Isolated builds with continuous proving (Delivery Orchestrator Act-3 unchanged plus four new standing rules: contract-first dispatch, isolation doctrine, continuous family-E2E cadence, bounded build-state/no sprawl), Phase 2.5 Product Acceptance E2E (Houston-pattern Playwright/Clerk journeys), and Phase 3 Integration hardening and cutover. It also carries a 'Memory Gap-Closure (epic set)' section that folds the memory-competitive-gap PRD/spine (g9vWbSYplh/iiCcKhGptV/vBvVDFklZo) into the standing pipeline rather than running it as a separate phase — i.e. this page is already actively functioning as the consolidation point for at least one other plan.
overlaps: This IS the plan of record the task context names as canonical — no other page competes with it; it delegates execution to reusable CURRENT-MACHINERY pages (orchestrator prompts yXUWpQpRjx/Ng66su4dVG/ErYdXzIj6g, Act-3 Delivery Orchestrator gkkUDzn277, Foundation Orchestrator UKanXYLCQD, SE-Arch gate 8sYi523i4t) rather than duplicating them. Direct overlap/confusion risk: the new local parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service, contract-tag unlock) is conceptually a refinement of THIS page's own Phase 2 'Contract-first dispatch rule' (a story is frontier-eligible only if its service's contract tag >= the tag its definition pack names) and the Phase-2 build-wave/WIP-cap machinery — it should NOT become a sibling/competing plan page. It belongs folded either into the Phase-2 orchestrator prompt (Ng66su4dVG, since Phase 2 is literally 'Isolated builds with continuous proving') or landed as an explicit child page under this program plan's Phase-2 section, with this page's Phase-2 bullet updated to cross-reference it. This page also already demonstrates the correct fold-in pattern via its own 'Memory Gap-Closure — phase placement' section (explicitly says 'not a standalone phase — it enters the standing pipeline'), which is the template to reuse for the parallel-build-plan fold-in.
